step_by_step_reasoning: The user starts from the web/mobile client, which targets CloudFront. Lambda@Edge handles traffic splitting between the legacy API gateway and the new Amazon API Gateway. On the new path, API Gateway triggers a custom authorizer (Lambda) which validates scopes against DynamoDB. For container services, API Gateway routes through an ELB to an Nginx proxy to handle mutual TLS, which then passes traffic to a private ALB fronting the backend container services.

graph.name: Accolade API Gateway Migration and Security Architecture
graph.notes: Migration architecture from legacy XML API gateway to Amazon API Gateway. Uses CloudFront and Lambda@Edge for traffic shifting, custom Lambda authorizers with DynamoDB for scope validation, and Nginx for mutual TLS termination before a private ALB.

nodes.name: User
nodes.notes: Web and mobile clients sending API requests.

nodes.name: CloudFront
nodes.notes: CDN caching and entry point for routing.

nodes.name: Lambda@Edge
nodes.notes: Handles traffic shifting between legacy gateway and AWS API Gateway.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Centralized, secure gateway natively integrating with Lambda.

nodes.name: Custom Authorizer Lambda
nodes.notes: Decodes JWT tokens and verifies scopes.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: Externalized store for API scope configurations.

nodes.name: ELB
nodes.notes: Load balances traffic from API Gateway to Nginx.

nodes.name: Nginx Proxy
nodes.notes: Terminates mutual TLS connection from API Gateway.

edges.notes: Initiates API request with JWT token.
Routes legacy traffic to legacy API.
Routes migrated traffic to Amazon API Gateway.
Invokes custom authorizer to validate token.
Routes validated request to backend cluster.
Forwards to Nginx proxy for mutual TLS.
Sends requests to the private ALB.
